name: Santa Comba de Rossas and Pinela Circular
name_original: Visto de Pinela e Serapicos — circular desde Santa Comba de Rossas
description: Cycle 31.4 miles (50.6 km) on this moderate road cycling route from Santa Comba de Rossas, featuring scenic views of Pinela and Serapicos.
region_intro: Road cycling around Santa Comba De Rossas, located in the municipality of Bragança, Portugal, offers routes through a tranquil rural landscape. The area is characterized by its natural beauty and proximity to significant features like the Albufeira do Azibo, a protected landscape. Road cycling routes in this region often feature varied terrain with notable elevation changes, traversing through natural parks and scenic countryside. The Bragança district provides a setting for cycling that includes both challenging climbs and more moderate paths. Best road cycling routes around Santa Comba De Rossas The most popular road cycling route is Santa Comba de Rossas and Pinela Circular, a 31.4 miles (50.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural surroundings of Santa Comba de Rossas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Outeiro and the Castle – Gimonde loop from Salsas, a difficult 63.2 miles (101.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and offers views towards Outeiro and a castle. Local road cyclists also love the Albufeira do Azibo – Macedo de Cavaleiros loop from Salsas, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through the protected landscape around Albufeira do Azibo, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Comba De Rossas is defined by rural landscapes, natural parks, and the Albufeira do Azibo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Comba De Rossas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Santa Comba De Rossas?
A: The road cycling routes around Santa Comba De Rossas feature a varied terrain, characterized by tranquil rural landscapes and significant elevation changes. You'll find paths traversing through natural parks and scenic countryside, offering both challenging climbs and more moderate sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a moderate ride?
A: Yes, Santa Comba De Rossas offers several moderate road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1400157404/santa-comba-de-rossas-and-pinela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Comba de Rossas and Pinela Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.6 km route with about 850 meters of elevation gain, ideal for a moderate challenge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo – Macedo de Cavaleiros loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.4 km long with around 623 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Outeiro and the Castle – Gimonde loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 101.7 km. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32137871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agrochão – Scenic Road to Nogueira loop from Antigo apeadeiro e posto do guarda da passagem de nível de Remisquedo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 97 km with over 1300 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Santa Comba De Rossas?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1400157404/santa-comba-de-rossas-and-pinela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Comba de Rossas and Pinela Circular&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Outeiro and the Castle – Gimonde loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo – Macedo de Cavaleiros loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt;, all designed as loops to bring you back to your starting point.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling near Santa Comba De Rossas?
A: The region offers beautiful natural features and points of interest. You can cycle towards the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt;, a protected landscape known for its river beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4947769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira River Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer scenic views such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña Mourisca&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montesinho Hills&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, some routes may pass by local points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Outeiro and the Castle – Gimonde loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt; offers views towards a castle. You might also encounter charming village churches, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Michael of Soutelo Mourisco&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Comba De Rossas?
A: The Bragança district, where Santa Comba De Rossas is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making for more comfortable rides through the rural landscapes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Santa Comba De Rossas area?
A: There are several road cycling routes available around Santa Comba De Rossas, with a total of 6 routes detailed in this guide. These include a mix of moderate and difficult options to suit various skill levels.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Santa Comba De Rossas?
A: Over 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain around Santa Comba De Rossas. They often highlight the tranquil rural landscapes, the presence of natural parks, and the scenic beauty of the Albufeira do Azibo area as key attractions for road cycling.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Albufeira do Azibo?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo – Macedo de Cavaleiros loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that leads through the protected landscape around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4886151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira do Azibo&lt;/a&gt;, allowing cyclists to experience this impressive body of water and its surroundings.

Q: What is the typical length and elevation gain for road cycling routes here?
A: Routes vary significantly. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1400157404/santa-comba-de-rossas-and-pinela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Comba de Rossas and Pinela Circular&lt;/a&gt; are around 50 km with 850 meters of elevation. More difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Outeiro and the Castle – Gimonde loop from Salsas&lt;/a&gt;, can be over 100 km with more than 1500 meters of climbing, offering substantial challenges.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route is approximately 50.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 39 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily paved, making it well-suited for road bikes. The surfaces are generally easy to cycle on, though it does feature an elevation gain of about 850 meters, indicating some challenging climbs.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for all fitness levels?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this route requires a good level of training and fitness due to its distance and significant elevation gain. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists rather than beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Santa Comba de Rossas. While specific parking details are not provided, as a recognized starting point, it is generally expected that parking options would be available in or near the settlement.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is named for the 'View of Pinela and Serapicos,' suggesting notable vistas of these areas. The broader region is known for picturesque landscapes, often traversing riverside and agricultural areas, as well as the scenic slopes of the Dão River.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features the settlement of Santa Comba de Rossas as its starting point and includes the scenic View of Pinela.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations are not detailed, road cycling routes in this region are generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring less predictable weather.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with or cross other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails, including the Ecopista do Tua, Rota dos Castanheiros em Flor, Por Trilhos das Minas da Ribeira, and Linha do Tua.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public areas, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly trail' in the same way a hiking path might be. If cycling with a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a trailer or basket and that local regulations are followed.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Santa Comba de Rossas, where you may find some basic amenities. However, as it's a rural cycling route, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as dedicated cafes or water stops might be infrequent along the majority of the path.
